posts.text:
Powerful.


Tevin Macharia Mukabana (@TevinMacharia)

Josh Howerton, pastor of Lakepointe Church, urged cohabiting couples during a sermon to honor God by getting married.

After the message, 52 couples came forward, met with pastors for counseling, and were married together in a joint ceremony.

The church later celebrated the couples, with more than 1,000 people attending a service to applaud their decision to repent and commit to marriage.





— https://nitter.net/TevinMacharia/status/203281

Probably need to outlaw social media anonymity. That's been my gut feeling for years. But I haven't thought through all the consequences of that.


PeeNutGallery (@PeeNutGallery42)

Might just be me, but I feel like with this new day and age of constant misinformation - it kind of sullys or lessens the impact of 1A. posts.text: I'm anti-misinformation and the amplification of it. However, we can survive misinformation and its amplification. COVID proved that. What we cannot survive is infringement on free speech.
posts.quote: I'm anti-misinformation and the amplification of it. However, we can survive misinformation and its amplification. COVID proved that. What we cannot survive is infringement on free speech.

posts.text:
I think we should ALL be careful here. No one knows the motive(s) of the poster sharing the bad information. 

I'm an old newspaper person. I loved the "good old days" when news moved slower. That's why I'm not the best at social media. I don't mind waiting. 

Slippery slope to justify the outlawing of free speech. 

Laws are rewritten off emotion and emotional events. Politicians need victims to rewrite the Constitution. Life at the top of political power is messy and dangerous. There are virtu
